title: canthium arboreum
intro: Canthium arboreum, also known as 'koko tree' or 'wild orange', is a tropical tree native to Africa, characterized by its vibrant orange or yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Canthium arboreum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Canthium arboreum is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
